source_pr.title: test: fix tests which may fail because of concurrency
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?


Problem Summary:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17380469/79364394-e3562500-7f7b-11ea-8521-e72a5b5de489.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17380469/79364451-f49f3180-7f7b-11ea-8e6c-7ccc63f702b1.png)

### What is changed and how it works?

Try to drop the table before creating it.


### Related changes


### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->


- No code

Side effects



### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

symptoms: PR title: tests may fail because of concurrency.
failure_signature: PR title: tests may fail because of concurrency.
root_cause_explanation: These tests were flaky due to cross-test interference on shared schema objects: running in parallel, other tests could leave behind tables with the same names, causing CREATE TABLE statements to fail. The fix proactively drops the relevant tables (DROP TABLE IF EXISTS ...) before creating them, ensuring isolation even when other tests run concurrently.
fix_pattern: Add `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS` before `CREATE TABLE` in affected tests to avoid cross-test table-name collisions under parallel execution.

evidence: evidence: PR title: tests may fail because of concurrency.
evidence: adds drop table if exists before create in several tests to avoid cross-test interference (expression/integration_test.go).

analysis:
